mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating consumer prices signal demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive RSAFS and CPIAUCSL growth yields positive SPX 60d returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in industrial production indicate immediate supply chain bottlenecks and earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during high-volatility regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5d SPX returns when market volatility indices exceed historical 80th percentile thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative SPX 20d returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.1 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where HOUST stays below 1100.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside a flat or rising federal funds rate indicate real rates are tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth combined with non-declining FEDFUNDS yields positive SPX 20d returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% during late-cycle expansions signals overheating that precedes central bank intervention, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 3.0% consistently predicts positive SPX 60d returns during late-cycle phases over a 10-year backtest.
